question_text: Name the three classes of levers and give one everyday example of a Class 1 lever.
correct_answer: The three classes are: Class 1 (pivot in the middle), Class 2 (load in the middle), and Class 3 (effort in the middle). Example of Class 1: a seesaw, crowbar, scissors, or plank over a rock.
explanation: Levers are grouped into three classes based on the position of the pivot (fulcrum), the effort (force applied), and the load (object being moved). A seesaw is a common Class 1 lever because the pivot is between the two people pushing down.
